2604.27551.title: Beyond the Training Distribution: Mapping Generalization Boundaries in Neural Program Synthesis
2604.27551.paper_summary: Large-scale transformers achieve impressive results on program synthesis benchmarks, yet their true generalization capabilities remain obscured by data contamination and opaque training corpora. To rigorously assess whether models are truly generalizing or merely retrieving memorized templates, we introduce a strictly controlled program synthesis environment based on a domain-specific arithmetic grammar. By systematically enumerating and evaluating millions of unique programs, we construct interpretable syntactic and semantic metric spaces. This allows us to precisely map data distributions and sample train and test splits that isolate specific distributional shifts. Our experiments demonstrate that optimizing density generalization -- through diverse sampling over both semantic and syntactic spaces -- induces robust out-of-distribution generalization. Conversely, evaluating support generalization reveals that transformers severely struggle with extrapolation, experiencing a performance drop of over 30% when forced to generate syntactically novel programs. While steadily scaling up compute improves generalization, the gains follow a strictly log-linear relationship. We conclude that robust generalization requires maximizing training diversity across multiple manifolds, and our findings indicate the necessity for novel search-based approaches to break through current log-linear scaling bottlenecks.
2604.27551.paper_summary_zh: 大型Transformer在程式合成基準測試中取得了令人印象深刻的結果，但它們真正的泛化能力仍然被數據污染和不透明的訓練語料所掩蓋。為了嚴格評估模型是否真正泛化或僅僅是檢索記憶模板，我們引入了一個基於特定領域的算術文法的嚴格控制的程式合成環境。通過系統性地列舉和評估數百萬個獨特的程式，我們構建了可解釋的語法和語義度量空間。這使我們能夠精確地映射數據分佈並抽樣訓練和測試分割，以隔離特定的分佈變化。我們的實驗表明，通過在語義和語法空間上進行多樣化抽樣來優化密度泛化，可以誘導出穩健的分佈外泛化。相反，評估支持泛化顯示Transformer在外推方面嚴重掙扎，當被迫生成語法上新穎的程式時，性能下降超過30%。雖然穩步增加計算能力改善了泛化，但增益遵循嚴格的對數線性關係。我們得出結論，穩健的泛化需要在多個流形上最大化訓練多樣性，我們的發現表明需要新的基於搜索的方法來突破當前的對數線性擴展瓶頸。
2604.27551.author: Henrik Voigt et.al.
2604.27551.authors: Henrik Voigt, Michael Habeck, Joachim Giesen
